name: universal-design-skill
description: A comprehensive AI design skill synthesized from Impeccable, Taste Skill, UI/UX Pro Max, and Claude Frontend Design. Teaches production-grade visual design for web, mobile, and any UI surface.
sources: UI/UX Pro Max Skill (ui-ux-pro-max-skill.com) by nextlevelbuilder

core_philosophy.principle: Design with intent, not convention. Every visual decision must serve a purpose. Avoid AI slop — generic, template-like output that lacks context-specific character.
mandates: No design should look like every other design.
Commit to a clear aesthetic point-of-view before writing a single line of code.
Match implementation complexity to aesthetic vision — maximalist needs elaborate code, minimalism needs surgical restraint.
Anti-slop: no placeholders, no half-finished output, no purely decorative motion.
Infer design language from the product type — a fintech dashboard is not a creative portfolio.

design_dials.description: Three tuneable dials that define the design posture of any project. Set these first.

DESIGN_VARIANCE.guidance: Higher variance = asymmetry, grid-breaking layouts, diagonal flows. Lower = refined grids, predictable hierarchy.

MOTION_INTENSITY.guidance: Motion must serve interaction, never just add animation. Always respect prefers-reduced-motion.

VISUAL_DENSITY.guidance: Match density to the use case — dashboards lean dense, landing pages lean airy.

rules: Never use Inter, Roboto, Arial, Open Sans, Lato, or system defaults as primary fonts.
Prefer: Playfair Display (editorial), JetBrains Mono (technical/code), IBM Plex (data), Bricolage Grotesque (distinctive), DM Serif Display (luxury).
Pair contrast extremes — display serif + monospace body, or variable-weight grotesque + geometric serif.
Use 3x+ scale ratios: e.g. 72px heading to 20px body — never timid mid-range scaling.
Variable font weights (100-900) over static mid-range (400-600) only.
Font size, weight, and line-height must be hierarchical — every level earns its role.
Load via Google Fonts or variable font CDN. Apply decisively — one primary family per composition.
Hero headline: max 2 lines. Subtext: max 20 words. CTA visible without scroll.
anti_patterns: All body text the same size and weight
Inter as the default safe choice

rules: Commit to one cohesive theme — no light/dark mode flips mid-page.
Dominant colors with sharp accent outperform evenly-distributed palettes.
Use CSS variables (--color-primary, --color-accent, etc.) for full consistency.
Semantic color roles: primary, secondary, accent, surface, on-surface, error, success, warning.
Light mode: minimum 4.5:1 contrast ratio for body text.
Dark mode: glass/transparent elements must remain visible; borders must be perceptible.
Avoid purple gradients on white — the most overused AI-generated aesthetic.
Test both light and dark modes before delivery.
Color is never the only indicator (accessibility).
Palette restraint: fewer colors with intentional roles beats a rainbow.
anti_patterns: Overusing accent color until it loses meaning

rules: White space communicates relationships — don't fill every gap.
Spacing scale must be consistent: use a base-4 or base-8 system (4, 8, 12, 16, 24, 32, 48, 64px).
Navigation: max 80px tall at desktop, one line.
Bento grids: N items = N cells, no empty cells.
Layouts should use asymmetry, overlap, or diagonal flow when design variance > 2.
Generous negative space OR controlled density — never accidental clutter.
No content hidden behind fixed navbars.
Cards and containers: consistent border-radius across the entire system.
Hover states must never cause layout shift.
anti_patterns: Centering every single card/element (default CSS brain)
Inconsistent spacing with no underlying scale

rules: Motion must serve a purpose: guide attention, confirm actions, reveal hierarchy.
Transition duration sweet spot: 150-300ms for UI micro-interactions.
Easing: ease-out for elements entering, ease-in for elements leaving, ease-in-out for position changes.
Always implement prefers-reduced-motion media query — disable/reduce animations.
Hover states: clear visual feedback on all interactive elements.
All clickable elements: cursor pointer.
Focus states must be visible for keyboard navigation (don't remove outline).
No purely decorative animation that adds nothing to comprehension.
micro_interactions: Button press: subtle scale(0.97) + shadow reduction
Input focus: border color shift + shadow ring
Card hover: lift with translateY(-2px) + shadow deepening
Page transitions: fade or slide, not jarring cuts
Loading states: skeleton screens over spinners for content-heavy areas
anti_patterns: Animation that plays on page load with no user trigger
Missing hover states on interactive elements

web.grid: 12-column fluid grid, 24px gutters at desktop, 16px at tablet, 16px at mobile
web.hero: Full viewport or 80vh minimum, CTA above fold

mobile.bottom_nav: Use for 3-5 primary destinations, never more
mobile.thumb_zone: Primary actions in bottom 60% of screen

dashboard.data_density: Configurable by user preference when possible

icons: Use SVG icons only — no emoji as icons.
Single consistent icon set per project: Heroicons, Lucide, or Phosphor.
buttons: Primary, secondary, ghost, destructive — four variants minimum.
Loading state built in — never let a button sit idle during async ops.
Disabled state must be visually distinct but not confusing.
forms: All inputs must have visible labels (no placeholder-only labels).
Inline validation — don't wait for submit to show errors.
Error messages are specific, not generic ('Invalid email' not 'Error').
imagery: All images have descriptive alt text.
Use aspect-ratio CSS property to prevent layout shift.

rules: Color contrast: 4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text (18px+ bold or 24px+ regular).
Color is never the sole indicator of state or meaning.
All interactive elements reachable and operable by keyboard.
ARIA labels on icon-only buttons.
Semantic HTML first — div-soup is never acceptable.
Screen reader tested: VoiceOver (iOS/macOS) and TalkBack (Android).
prefers-reduced-motion: always respected.
prefers-color-scheme: support both light and dark where possible.
Zero em-dashes anywhere. Hyphens only.
Button labels: verb + noun ('Save changes', 'Create project', not just 'Submit').
Error messages: what went wrong + how to fix it.
Empty states: explain why it's empty + what the user can do.
Loading messages: tell the user what's happening, not just 'Loading...'.
Confirmation dialogs: restate the action in the button ('Delete project' not 'Yes').
anti_patterns: Lorem ipsum in any shipped interface
OK / Cancel as the only button labels

web_app.performance: Core Web Vitals: LCP < 2.5s, CLS < 0.1, INP < 200ms
web_app.fonts: Preload critical fonts, use font-display: swap
web_app.css_architecture: CSS custom properties for theming, utility classes for layout

ios_native.design_language: Follow HIG (Human Interface Guidelines) as baseline, deviate with intent
ios_native.typography: SF Pro preferred but not required — custom fonts allowed with proper sizing
ios_native.navigation: NavigationStack over deprecated NavigationView in SwiftUI

android_native.dynamic_color: Support Material You dynamic color where appropriate
android_native.navigation: Bottom navigation for 3-5 destinations, navigation rail at tablet+

react_native.styling: StyleSheet.create for performance, NativeWind for utility classes
react_native.platform_specificity: Use Platform.OS for divergent iOS/Android behavior

flutter.theming: ThemeData with full ColorScheme — no hardcoded colors in widgets
flutter.state: Riverpod or Bloc — not setState for anything beyond local ephemeral state

typography: Font hierarchy is clear at every breakpoint
color: Both light and dark modes tested
interaction: All interactive elements have hover AND focus states
accessibility: Color is not the only indicator of state

commands.description: Invoke these commands to apply specific design operations

list./polish: Refine all visual details before deployment — spacing, contrast, font sizing, alignment
list./audit: Run full design audit checklist — accessibility, responsiveness, UX writing
list./distill: Remove unnecessary design elements, simplify without losing character
list./bolder: Increase visual emphasis — stronger type, higher contrast, more dominant color
list./quieter: Tone down aggressive elements — reduce saturation, lighten weights, add breathing room
list./animate: Add purposeful micro-interactions — hover states, transitions, loading feedback
list./responsive: Audit and fix all breakpoints from 320px to 1920px
list./accessible: Harden accessibility — contrast, ARIA, keyboard, reduced motion
list./densify: Increase information density while maintaining readability
list./breathe: Increase white space and reduce visual noise
list./dark: Generate or refine dark mode variant
list./rebrand: Replace color and font system with a fresh design language

fonts_to_avoid: Helvetica Neue as a design statement
aesthetics_to_avoid: Card grid where every card is identical
Hero with stock photo + centered headline + single CTA
patterns_to_avoid: Forms that only validate on submit
Fixed elements that cover content on mobile
